files_modified.change_summary: Changed 'Resolves #{{ISSUE_ID}}' to 'Closes #{{ISSUE_ID}}' to use the GitHub closing keyword that guarantees auto-close on merge. The 'Resolves' keyword also works, but 'Closes' is the most explicit and widely recognized. Added a blank line after the keyword for clarity.

Updated Rule 5 from 'Resolves #<issue_id>' to 'Closes #<issue_id>' to match the updated template. Added a new Rule 9 explicitly instructing the PR assembler to always include a 'Closes #<issue_id>' line in the PR body on its own line so GitHub's auto-close detection reliably parses it. Added a note that the closing keyword must appear in the final rendered PR description (not just a comment or code block) to ensure GitHub processes it.

No functional changes needed. The script already correctly receives and propagates the ISSUE_ID variable. The issue number is available in the forge workspace via the .forge-meta directory and is consumed downstream by the pipeline agents that fill in the PR template. Reviewed and confirmed the issue number flow is intact.

No changes needed. The config already specifies cleanup_after_merge: true and the branch/label conventions. There is no missing configuration for issue-to-PR linkage; the linkage is handled entirely by the PR description template and the assembler agent rules.
